hero.lede: Product analytics you actually run yourself. Ingest events, build funnels, slice cohorts — without shipping your users to a third-party warehouse.
hero.meta.stack.label: built with
hero.meta.deploy.label: runs on
hero.meta.license.label: source
hero.meta.status.label: status
theater.title: Watch events become decisions.
theater.lede: Three minute-long stories. Click a tab — or just watch them rotate.
theater.ingest.title: Events stream in. The pipeline never blocks.
theater.ingest.lede: Async ingest workers absorb spikes. Validation happens at the edge, persistence happens in the background, your client gets a 202 in single-digit milliseconds.
theater.funnel.title: Drop the steps. The funnel builds itself.
theater.funnel.lede: No SQL gymnastics. Pick events, set the order, watch conversion rates fall into place — segmented by anything you've ever sent as a property.
theater.cohort.title: Slice retention by anything you tracked.
theater.cohort.lede: Plan, country, device, referrer — every property becomes a dimension. Heatmaps update as the data lands.
features.title: Six things you'd otherwise glue together.
features.ingest.title: Async event ingestion
features.ingest.body: Buffered writes, schema validation, idempotency keys. Survives traffic spikes without dropping.
features.funnels.title: Conversion funnels
features.funnels.body: Multi-step funnels with property-level segmentation. Time-to-convert distributions included.
features.analytics.title: Live analytics
features.analytics.body: Charts that update as events land. Slice by any property you've ever tracked.
features.cohorts.title: Cohort distributions
features.cohorts.body: Retention heatmaps, behavioral cohorts, custom segments. Everything queryable from SQL too.
features.gdpr.title: GDPR primitives
features.gdpr.body: Right-to-erasure endpoints, audit logs, PII tagging. Data subject requests stop being ad hoc.
features.selfhost.title: Self-hosted, by default
features.selfhost.body: Docker image, compose file, DigitalOcean App Platform spec. Your data, your VPC, your bill.
pricing.title: Pay for what you run, not per seat.
pricing.lede: Two tiers. No "contact sales." Self-host is always free.
pricing.plan.self.name: Self-hosted
pricing.plan.self.lede: Run it on your own infrastructure. No event limits, no feature gates.
pricing.plan.self.note: We respond within 48 h via GitHub Issues.
pricing.plan.cloud.name: Managed cloud
pricing.plan.cloud.lede: We run it for you on AInvirion infrastructure. SLA, backups, upgrades.
docs.title: Run the whole stack in 60 seconds.
docs.lede: One Postgres, one FastAPI, one container network. The way you'd ship it yourself.
docs.specs.title: // targets
docs.banner.title: Quickstart
docs.banner.text: Full docs ship with v1.0 — this page is the quickstart.
faq.title: Things you actually want to know.
faq.q1.q: Can I really run this on my own box?
faq.q1.a: Yes. There's a Dockerfile, a docker-compose.yml, and a DigitalOcean App Platform spec. Bring Postgres 16 and a small VM and you're running.
faq.q2.q: Why not just use PostHog or Mixpanel?
faq.q2.a: Because sometimes you can't ship user identifiers to a third party — and "self-hosted PostHog" has a footprint we couldn't justify for a small team. Product Telemetry is FastAPI + Postgres. That's it.
faq.q3.a: A single REST endpoint with an API key header. Wrap it in 12 lines of whatever language you're in. We ship thin TS and Python helpers, no obligation to use them.
faq.q4.a: Source-available under NDA, not OSS. You can read, run, and modify it for your own use. Redistribution and sublicensing are restricted. The license file in the repo is authoritative — you can review the key terms before requesting access.
faq.q5.q: How do I report a bug or get help?
faq.q5.a: Open an issue on the AInvirion support repo on GitHub. That's the one and only support channel — no email, no Discord, no Slack workspace to lose track of.
faq.q6.q: What happens to my data on the managed plan?
faq.q6.a: It lives in a dedicated Postgres instance on AInvirion infrastructure (Seattle or Santiago region). Daily encrypted backups, 30-day retention, GDPR data subject endpoints exposed.
cta.title: Ship your own analytics this week.
cta.lede: Spin up the stack in an afternoon. Start sending events tonight.
